chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P.NO.S-1560 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) For katcha peshi. 25.09.2017 Mr. Ghulam Nabi Meo, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Ghulam Abbas Sangi, Assistant A.G a/w DSP Ayoob Dars City Mirpurkhas and Abid Noon SHO City Mirpurkhas. = With reference to the notice of the present case, Mr. Nouman Raja Khan, Advocate files power on behalf of the respondents No.13 to 15 which is taken on record. He submits that the private respondents neither harassed the petitioner nor they intend to harass in future. Learned Assistant A.G files parawise comments on behalf of the respondents No.3, 4, 5, 6, 7 and 8, which are also taken on record and copies hereof have been supplied to the petitioner’s Counsel. Learned Counsel for the petitioner has filed an application under Section 151 CPC whereby he seeks appointment of commissioner to inspect the subject property of the petitioner to ascertain the situation of the site as according to the learned Counsel after the notice received of the present petition, the poultry shades of the petitioner existed at land in question have been demolished. Be that as it may, from the comments of the official respondents it appears that the dispute between the petitioner and the private respondents is of private nature against which the civil case is pending before the competent Court of Law. In view of the above, since the petition has very limited scope, therefore, this petition is being disposed of with direction to the private respondents not to cause any harassment to the petitioner with further direction

Be that as it may, from the comments of the official respondents it appears that the dispute between the petitioner and the private respondents is of private nature against which the civil case is pending before the competent Court of Law. In view of the above, since the petition has very limited scope, therefore, this petition is being disposed of with direction to the private respondents not to cause any harassment to the petitioner with further direction to the official respondents to discharge their functions and duties strictly in accordance with law in a fair and reasonable manner without patronizing any group or individuals. The petitioner and the private respondents are at liberty to seek remedy of their grievances, if any, before the appropriate forum by invoking due process of law and not by taking law in their hands. Since the private respondents No.10 to 12 are not in attendance, therefore, the concerned SHO is also directed to serve the copy of this order on the above named private respondents by calling them in Police Station and compliance of such service should be furnished with the Additional Registrar of this Court. JUDGE Shahid
